elements.name: Green docs PR
elements.documentation: The reviewed OpenWiki-only change is pushed and its documentation pull request has passed applicable final checks.

elements.name: Revalidate PR
elements.documentation: Recheck that the pull request is open, mergeable, clean, on the expected branches, at the reviewed head, and confined to the permitted documentation scope.

elements.name: PR eligible?
elements.documentation: Only an exact, green, documentation-only pull request may enter the autonomous merge path; uncertainty or drift blocks it.

elements.name: Merge blocked
elements.documentation: Stop without merging when checks, identity, scope, or pull-request state are not proven safe.

elements.name: Check target freshness
elements.documentation: Fetch main and read the pull request base SHA immediately before merge, comparing both with the immutable target captured for this run.

elements.name: Target current?
elements.documentation: The merge may continue only while both remote main and the pull request base still equal the frozen target.

elements.name: Regenerate from main
elements.documentation: A stale documentation result is superseded and regenerated from the newly landed main state rather than merged or queued.

elements.name: Build verified merge
elements.documentation: Create a two-parent merge commit without moving local refs, using the reviewed documentation tree and the frozen target and reviewed head as parents, then verify its tree and parent order.

elements.name: Push once to main
elements.documentation: Publish the verified merge commit with one ordinary non-force push; Git and branch protection reject a stale or disallowed update.

elements.name: Push accepted?
elements.documentation: Separate a successful atomic publication from a refusal that must be inspected rather than bypassed or blindly retried.

elements.name: Inspect refusal
elements.documentation: Fetch and inspect the new remote state after a refused push to distinguish a superseding main advance from another unresolved failure.

elements.name: Target advanced?
elements.documentation: A concurrent main advance supersedes this generated result; any other unresolved refusal preserves evidence and stops.

elements.name: Regenerate from main
elements.documentation: A concurrent main winner supersedes the stale documentation result and requires regeneration from landed state.

elements.name: Preserve evidence and stop
elements.documentation: Do not retry around the guard when the refusal is not explained by a stale target; retain evidence for operator direction.

elements.name: Verify landing
elements.documentation: After the push succeeds, confirm GitHub reports the pull request merged and fetched main equals the constructed merge commit.

elements.name: OpenWiki merged
elements.documentation: The documentation update is landed and the dedicated maintainer continues observing future main advances.

flows.documentation: A green documentation pull request enters final remote-state validation.
Evaluate the freshly observed checks, identity, and scope together.

flows.name: yes
flows.documentation: An exact, clean, green, in-scope pull request proceeds to the last freshness guard.

flows.name: no
flows.documentation: Any unrelated path, failed or pending check, changed head, or uncertain state blocks merge.
Compare fetched main and the REST-reported pull request base with the immutable run target.

flows.name: yes
flows.documentation: Only a still-current target permits construction of the merge commit.

flows.name: no
flows.documentation: An advanced base or main supersedes this result and returns maintenance to generation from landed state.
A merge object is published only after its reviewed tree and parent identities are verified.
The ordinary non-force push is the atomic race and branch-protection guard.

flows.name: yes
flows.documentation: A successful push must still be confirmed through GitHub and a fresh fetch.

flows.name: no
flows.documentation: A refused update is inspected without force, bypass, or a retry around the guard.
The fetched state determines whether the generated result became stale or another failure remains unresolved.

flows.name: yes
flows.documentation: A concurrent main winner supersedes the stale documentation result.

flows.name: no
flows.documentation: An unresolved non-stale refusal preserves its evidence and stops for direction.
Confirmed GitHub merge state and remote main identity complete the guarded publication.
